purpose: Help the user pick a restaurant fast, with neighborhood context, key chips (price, cuisine, vibe), and quick booking detail.
primaryUserGoal: Decide where to eat tonight (or for a specific occasion) and see what's worth booking.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks for restaurant recommendations, dinner options, where to eat, or a shortlist of places in a neighborhood.
goodFor: Tonight's dinner in a specific city or neighborhood
Restaurant shortlists for a visiting friend
supports: Filter chips for party size, time, cuisine, price
Grouped list of restaurants by neighborhood
Detail panel with hours, address, price chips, cuisine tags
idealDataShape: Filter context (date, time, party size, cuisine)
Per-restaurant details: hours, address, price, cuisine, booking availability

populationInstructions.summary: Start with filter chips (date/time/party size/cuisine/price). Group 5-7 restaurants by neighborhood on the left. Populate a detail-panel on the right for the initially selected restaurant with hours, address, cuisine, price tier, and a photo query.
steps: Parse the user's implicit filters: city, neighborhood, cuisine, budget, time.
Emit filter chips so the user can see and refine them.
Group 5-7 restaurants by neighborhood; tone-code top picks with success.
Populate the detail-panel for the first restaurant with hours, price, address, and a photo image.query.
Keep per-item subtitle short (cuisine + price tier); put the full description in the detail panel.
Include an image.query for the detail-panel restaurant photo (e.g. '<restaurant name> interior').
guardrails: Don't make up hours or addresses — if unknown, say 'check website'.
Don't claim booking availability unless verified.
Keep to one cuisine-dense neighborhood per group.

patchPrefer: Use set_filter_chips when the user refines cuisine/time/price
Use upsert_groups to add or swap restaurants in a neighborhood
Use set_detail when the user picks a different restaurant
replaceTriggers: User switches from dinner to a different meal context
persistAcrossUpdates: filter chips selected by the user
